# Agentic Tooling: Compounding Wave Plan

Sequencing for closing the gaps found in the [gap-coverage audit](agentic-tooling-gap-audit.md), folded so each wave's win cheapens or unlocks the next rather than running 13 areas as independent parallel tracks. Ordered by leverage, not by area number. Every wave defaults to wiring an existing mechanism to the chokepoint that matters over building something new ‚Äî the audit's own finding was that almost nothing here needs invention.

## Why this order

Two items showed up as blockers across most of the 13 areas:

1. **Zero PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks exist anywhere** ‚Äî the reason destructive-command confirmation, secret-leak detection, and sovereign-tooling enforcement are all prose-only today (cited in Areas 6, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13).
2. **The sovereign-tooling mandate has no mechanical enforcement** ‚Äî `a0 xr deploy` skips the cost/dependency guardrails `a0 forge deploy` already has, and this has already been violated in production once (cited in Areas 7, 8, 9, 12, 13).

Everything below is sequenced so those two land first ‚Äî cheaply, since the pattern list, the guardrail function, and the confirm-token gate all already exist and just need a new call site ‚Äî and every later wave either reuses that hook infrastructure or reuses schema fields the earlier waves introduced.

## Wave 0 ‚Äî Prose fixes (no code risk, ships in an hour)

Pure documentation edits. Zero engineering risk, fully reversible, and they establish the vocabulary (rule names, reason strings) that Wave 1's hook will quote back to the agent.

- Fix the "never stop until complete" guardrail in AGENTS.md ‚Äî add carve-outs for an explicit human stop phrase, a genuine judgment call, and a Plan-Mode checkpoint before nontrivial edits (resolves 3 findings at once ‚Äî Area 11 #1).
- Rewrite CONTRIBUTING.md so `git commit/push --no-verify` is a logged exception path, not documented step 6‚Äì7 of the standard workflow (Area 13 #1).
- Add the "Verified: / Assuming:" and provenance-citation conventions to AGENTS.md's Intellectual Honesty section (Area 11 #5, #6).
- Run `pnpm run a0 -- agentfile sync` to propagate into CLAUDE.md.

## Wave 1 ‚Äî The root-cause hook

One PR, one file family (`.claude/settings.json` + one new hook script). This is the single highest-leverage fix in the whole audit.

- Add a **PreToolUse hook** that blocks/forces confirmation on destructive Bash patterns (`--force`, `rm -rf`, `reset --hard`, `clean -f`, raw `wrangler deploy`, raw `git commit`/`add -A` outside `gn flow`, raw `pnpm install`) ‚Äî reusing the pattern list already written in `cera-safety.ts`, with reason strings pulled from Wave 0's docs.
- Add a **PostToolUse hook** that regex-scans tool output for secret-shaped strings (AWS/GCP/JWT/Cloudflare-token) and redacts them, generalizing the one-off `redact_ucan_in_argv` pattern already in `moonshine/src/agent_runtime.rs`.
- Replace the blanket `"Bash"`/`"Agent"`/`"Task"`/`"WebFetch"`/`"WebSearch"` entries in `.claude/settings.local.json` with scoped, command-prefixed patterns matching the `Bash(bun run:*)`-style entries already in the same file.

**Unlocks:** every later wave that needs "block X" or "detect Y" gets a hook to attach to instead of building its own mechanism.

## Wave 2 ‚Äî Make the mandated deploy path enforce what it already claims to

All four items are "call an existing function from a new call site" ‚Äî no new logic.

- Wire `checkWranglerCostGuardrails` (and `auditDependencyPolicy`) into `a0 xr deploy`'s `runXRanger()` as a pre-flight, closing the gap where the *mandated* deploy path skips the guardrail `a0 forge deploy` already enforces.
- Flip `a0 audit *` to fail-closed by default (`--permissive` as the opt-out, not `--strict` as the opt-in).
- Wire `pnpm run a0 -- audit security` / `audit cloudflare --strict` into required CI, and remove `continue-on-error: true` from `scanner-gate.yml`.
- Wire `detectHardcodedSecrets()` into pre-commit (staged-diff) and make pre-push actually exit 1 on a hit instead of swallowing it.

**Unlocks:** the sovereign-tooling mandate becomes structurally true instead of prose-true ‚Äî this is the second root-cause item from the audit, and Wave 1's hook infrastructure makes the CI-side pieces of this wave cheap to add.

## Wave 3 ‚Äî Let tool schemas say what they already know

A plumbing fix, not new authoring work ‚Äî 167/170 leaf tools already declare this metadata; it's dropped at one aggregation boundary.

- Fix `toNetworkTool()`/`serializeTool()` in the MCP superserver to pass `tool.annotations` (`readOnlyHint`/`destructiveHint`/`idempotentHint`) through instead of dropping them.
- Add `reversibility` / `requiresConfirmation` / `reason` / `safetyClassification` fields to the shared `Tool` type.
- Add a `contractVersion`/`schemaVersion` field to the same type (root blocker for all deprecation/pinning/migration work later).

**Unlocks:** Wave 1's PreToolUse hook can be upgraded cheaply to check `destructiveHint`/`requiresConfirmation` from the tool schema itself instead of only pattern-matching Bash strings. This is the compounding step ‚Äî a later wave gets stronger because this one shipped first.

## Wave 4 ‚Äî Harden the actual dispatch chokepoint

The most invasive wave, so it's sequenced after the cheap wins are already banked. Uses Wave 3's new schema fields directly.

- Add one generic schema-validation step in `tools/call` dispatch (checks `args` against each tool's `inputSchema` before the handler runs) ‚Äî closes four separate findings across Areas 2 and 3 at once.
- Add a `{code, retryable, retryAfterMs}` error envelope, derived from the 4xx/5xx discrimination pattern already proven in `r2.ts`/`search-console.ts`.
- Add an optional `idempotencyKey` param + a short-TTL dedup table for mutating tools.
- Split `ToolInputError` vs `ToolExecutionError` into the correct JSON-RPC codes instead of collapsing both to `-32603`.

## Wave 5 ‚Äî Observability at the same chokepoint (nearly free)

Wave 4 already opens `runtime.ts`'s dispatch path ‚Äî adding a journal here is a marginal addition, not a new project.

- Emit `mcp.tool.call`/`mcp.tool.result` journal events from the same dispatch point.
- Add per-call telemetry (model, reasoning-effort, tokens, latency).
- Standardize the `truncated`/`maxBytes` signal across all tool results, not just `read_file`.

## Wave 6 ‚Äî Cost/budget tracking (uses Wave 5's telemetry)

- Add a token/$ budget field to `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate`, decremented via a control-plane hook.
- Extend `a0 show cost` (which already reads `events.jsonl`) to include token/$ spend ‚Äî the audit calls this "almost free from plumbing that already exists" once Wave 5's telemetry is landing.
- Add a preflight cost/token estimate before multi-agent fanout.

## Wave 7 ‚Äî Alerting (uses Wave 6's cost data and Wave 1's secret-detection hook)

- One unified alert channel (replacing the current "five dashboards" ‚Äî Cloudflare cost, GCP billing, `rejections.jsonl` ‚Äî with one aggregation point).
- A weekly scheduled digest (cost + security events) over the same channel.
- An idle-agent watchdog and a concurrency/total-spawn ceiling on `fireCannonLanes` (the one live, unaddressed safety gap in the production agent-spawn path).

---

Waves 0‚Äì2 are documentation- and wiring-only, touch no new abstractions, and each is small enough to land as one focused PR. Wave 3 onward touches the MCP superserver's core dispatch path directly and deserves a checkpoint before starting, given the blast radius.
